title: Oil ‘jumps’ | Europe’s ‘hottest June’ | China storms - Carbon Brief
gist: Carbon Brief's 9 July 2026 Daily Briefing digests a breakdown of the US-Iran ceasefire driving oil above $80 and Hormuz disruption, Western Europe's hottest June on record straining power grids, the UK's 20-year Sizewell B nuclear extension, El Niño-driven China storms, and analyst arguments that electrification and EVs are the best long-term insurance against oil shocks.

themes.theme: Oil shock from Hormuz and US-Iran breakdown

insights.claim: Oil prices rose briefly above $80 per barrel after Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire over, marking oil's biggest daily percentage rise since 1 May, driven by attacks on fossil-fuel tankers near the strait of Hormuz.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Hormuz disruption reintroduces a geopolitical risk premium into crude that traders should watch for tanker-flow escalation.
insights.quote: Oil prices rose briefly above $80 per barrel yesterday, after US president Donald Trump declared that the US-Iran ceasefire was "over"
insights.locator: Oil prices jump as US-Iran ceasefire breaks down

insights.claim: At least four oil and gas tankers turned back from trying to transit the strait of Hormuz according to ship-tracking data, hampering efforts to normalise oil and gas flows through the route.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Physical tanker reroutings are a real-time supply-tightness signal distinct from headline price moves.
insights.quote: At least four oil and gas tankers have turned back from trying to transit the strait, according to ship-tracking data
insights.locator: Oil prices jump as US-Iran ceasefire breaks down

insights.claim: Some market analysts are not expecting oil prices to climb much higher despite the ceasefire breakdown.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A muted upside view tempers the case for chasing crude on the Hormuz headlines.
insights.quote: Some market analysts are saying they're not expecting prices to climb much higher.
insights.locator: Oil prices jump as US-Iran ceasefire breaks down

insights.claim: Russia has banned diesel exports after Ukrainian drone attacks on its refineries triggered the country's worst fuel crisis since the collapse of the Soviet Union.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A Russian diesel export ban tightens global refined-product markets on top of the crude spike.
insights.quote: Russia has banned diesel exports after Ukrainian drone attacks on its refineries triggered the country's "worst fuel crisis since the collapse of the Soviet Union"

insights.claim: Vietnam is considering developing more coal-fired capacity to meet power needs as the US-Iran war constrains supplies of liquefied natural gas.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: LNG supply constraints pushing Asian buyers back toward coal is a second-order emissions and thermal-coal demand consequence of the Gulf conflict.
insights.quote: Vietnam is considering developing more coal-fired capacity to meet its power needs as the US-Iran war constrains supplies of liquefied natural gas.

insights.claim: The IMF expects the global economy to expand by a sluggish 3% in 2026, attributing the drag to the Iran war and AI.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A downgraded 3% global growth print frames the macro backdrop for energy and risk assets.
insights.quote: the International Monetary Fund expects the global economy to expand by a "sluggish" 3% in 2026, due to the Iran war and AI

insights.claim: Western Europe recorded its warmest June on record at an average 20.74C, more than 3C above the 1991-2020 June average, per the EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Record heat drives cooling demand and grid stress that ripples into power prices and utility reliability.
insights.quote: The average temperature in Western Europe last month was 20.74C, more ​than 3C above the average for June during 1991-2020
insights.locator: Western Europe records hottest June on record

insights.claim: Globally, June 2026 was 0.56C hotter than the 1991-2020 average and 1.39C hotter than preindustrial levels, the second-warmest June on record, with oceans hotter than scientists had ever seen.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Record ocean heat foreshadows extreme-weather intensity that raises physical climate risk across sectors.
insights.quote: Globally, June 2026 was 0.56C hotter than the 1991-2020 average and 1.39C hotter than preindustrial levels, making it the second-warmest June on record
insights.locator: Western Europe records hottest June on record

insights.claim: France faces up to five nuclear plants potentially curbing output in coming days as soaring temperatures affect the rivers used for cooling.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Heat-driven nuclear output curbs tighten European power supply exactly when demand peaks, a recurring summer trade.
insights.quote: in France, up to five nuclear plants "could face output curbs in the coming days as soaring temperatures affect rivers used for cooling"

insights.claim: The UK government granted Sizewell B a 20-year life extension, keeping it producing electricity until 2055 and extending its lifetime to 60 years to meet growing low-carbon electricity demand.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Life extensions of existing nuclear are a cheaper low-carbon baseload option than new builds and support the operator's cash flows.
insights.quote: The UK government has granted the Sizewell B nuclear plant a 20-year life extension, which will see it producing electricity until 2055
insights.locator: UK: Sizewell B nuclear power plant granted a 20-year life extension

insights.claim: Sizewell B's owners will sign a contract for difference receiving £70.50 per megawatt hour in 2025 prices, indexed to inflation, for the 20 years between 2035 and 2055.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: The £70.50/MWh CfD sets a revenue floor benchmark for valuing extended nuclear assets.
insights.quote: it will receive £70.50 per megawatt hour in 2025 prices "indexed to inflation, for the 20 years between 2035 and 2055"
insights.locator: UK: Sizewell B nuclear power plant granted a 20-year life extension

insights.claim: Global nuclear power is set to climb 44% worldwide with China topping the US, according to BNEF.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A 44% nuclear expansion with China leading reshapes uranium demand and reactor-vendor competitive positioning.
insights.quote: Nuclear power to climb 44% worldwide as China tops US, BNEF says

themes.theme: El Niño and China extreme weather

insights.claim: Scientists warn China's extreme weather will only grow more frequent in 2026 as climate change and the emergence of El Niño expose the country to destructive weather events.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Intensifying Chinese extreme weather threatens power supply stability and agricultural output, feeding commodity volatility.
insights.quote: Scientists warn that China's extreme weather will "only grow more frequent this year"
insights.locator: Catastrophic storms to test China's resilience in 2026

insights.claim: Xiamen University's Wu Wei says climate uncertainty is causing fluctuations in China's power supply this summer.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Climate-driven power fluctuations in China are a supply-side risk for its manufacturing and commodity demand.
insights.quote: climate uncertainty is causing fluctuations in China's power supply this summer
insights.locator: Catastrophic storms to test China's resilience in 2026

insights.claim: China's domestic auto sales fell 23.4% in June, continuing a nine-month trend, while exports jumped 82.1%.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Collapsing domestic auto demand alongside surging exports intensifies overcapacity and trade-tension concerns for Chinese automakers.
insights.quote: China's domestic auto sales fell 23.4% in June, continuing a nine-month trend, while exports jumped 82.1%

insights.claim: Bloomberg columnist David Fickling argues governments should divert oil-shock insurance spending from petroleum inventories toward EV subsidies and charging networks, calling electrification an under-appreciated alternative.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A framing of EV buildout as strategic energy security supports policy tailwinds for charging infrastructure and EV makers.
insights.quote: The best protection against the next oil shock is not having to use so much oil in the first place.
insights.locator: A better way to guard against the next oil crisis

insights.claim: China's battery-powered cars and trucks are currently displacing around 1.8m barrels a day of oil demand, per David Fickling.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: 1.8m b/d of displaced demand quantifies the structural bearish pressure EVs put on oil consumption.
insights.quote: China's battery-powered cars and trucks are currently displacing in the region of 1.8m barrels a day of oil demand
insights.locator: A better way to guard against the next oil crisis

insights.claim: IEA head Fatih Birol says the US-Israel-Iran conflict has shattered confidence in the strait of Hormuz and, combined with the loss of trust in Russian exports, is redrawing the global energy map according to who trusts whom.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A trust-based reordering of energy trade favors renewables, nuclear, EVs and batteries but could revive coal in some markets.
insights.quote: there is no going back to the way things were before the war began
insights.locator: The global energy map is being redrawn in real time

insights.claim: Guardian columnist George Monbiot argues incoming UK prime minister Andy Burnham should cancel the government's carbon-capture and storage programme.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Political pressure to scrap UK CCS funding is a policy risk for carbon-capture project developers.
insights.quote: incoming UK prime minister Andy Burnham should cancel the government's carbon-capture and storage programme
